name: Oxytetracycline
dosage: 10–20 mg/kg IM or IV, repeat every 24–48h per formulation (LA vs short-acting)
contraindications: Avoid in severe hepatic or renal impairment; avoid in late pregnancy where possible
withdrawal: Meat 28 days, Milk 96 hours (check label — LA formulations vary)
notes: Long-acting formulations reduce injection frequency; rotate injection sites for large volumes.

name: Meloxicam
dosage: Cattle: 0.5 mg/kg IV/SC single dose. Dog: 0.1–0.2 mg/kg SC/oral.
contraindications: Avoid in dehydrated or hypotensive animals; caution with concurrent nephrotoxic drugs; avoid in cats at cattle-label doses
notes: Give with adequate hydration. Do not combine with other NSAIDs or corticosteroids.

name: Ivermectin
dosage: 200 mcg/kg SC (injectable) or per pour-on label
contraindications: Avoid in certain dog breeds with MDR1 mutation (Collies) at high doses; not for lactating dairy animals unless label permits
withdrawal: Meat 35–49 days depending on formulation; not for use in lactating animals producing milk for human consumption unless labelled
notes: Rotate anthelmintic classes to manage resistance; do not use in severely debilitated animals without assessment.
